title: Odontostemma napuligerum
intro: Odontostemma napuligerum, also known as Chinese tooth-cup, is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. This plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Odontostemma napuligerum include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Odontostemma napuligerum is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures.
taxonomy: Odontostemma napuligerum is a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is native to China. This species was previously classified within the family Scrophulariaceae but was later reclassified to the family Plantaginaceae due to molecular phylogenetic studies. Odontostemma napuligerum belongs to the genus Odontostemma and the kingdom Plantae. The classification of Odontostemma napuligerum reflects the ongoing changes in plant taxonomy and the evolution of our understanding of plant relationships.
uses: Odontostemma napuligerum is reportedly used in traditional medicine, although its effectiveness and safety are not well-documented.
